utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all 5 corresponding top-level items. Now check if each is fully specified with all required details: (1) health_coverage: specifies physical and mental health insurance and provides a cost (€100/month) — fully specified. (2) property_coverage: specifies home/contents insurance and costs (€85/month total with breakdown €60 + €25) — fully specified. (3) travel_coverage: specifies travel insurance and costs (€130/month total with breakdown €50 + €80) — fully specified. (4) additional_services: lists enhancement services and provides a cost (€25/month) — fully specified. (5) total_budget: provides monthly and annual cost breakdown and itemized costs — fully specified. Therefore, all 5/5 items are included and fully detailed.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: Map each selected item from the final coverage plan to the closest matching option name in the ground-truth ratings and extract its numeric rating. Health: 'LifeFirst Standard Health Insurance' corresponds to 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' under primary_health_coverage.physical_health. Mental health is included in LifeFirst, but the ground truth has a separate 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' entry under mental_health as well; since it's explicitly referenced as included in LifeFirst, we map to that mental health plan rating. Property: 'VitalPlus Standard Home Content Insurance' matches 'VitalPlus Standard Coverage' under home_content. The 'Equipment gap protection add-on' is not an item in ground truth; skip. Travel: 'InsurePlus Standard Travel Insurance' matches 'InsurePlus Standard Plan' under travel_insurance. 'Adventure Travel Coverage' matches the travel_enhancement_services item 'Adventure Travel Coverage'. Additional services like professional liability/compliance are not in ratings; skip.
